title: Grafana Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Grafana** is an open-source observability + dashboarding platform with a REST HTTP API. This connector — `GrafanaConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "grafana"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Health | `/api/health` | Server reachability + database state probe |
| Org | `/api/org` | Current organization for the auth token |
| Dashboards | `/api/dashboards` + `/api/search` | List, get, create, delete dashboards |
| Folders | `/api/folders` | List + create folders |
| Datasources | `/api/datasources` + `/api/ds/query` | List, create, run queries |
| Alert Rules | `/api/v1/provisioning/alert-rules` | List provisioned alert rules |
| Users | `/api/users` | Paginated user list |
| Teams | `/api/teams/search` | Search teams |

The base URL is **tenant-specific** — `https://<stack>.grafana.net` (Grafana Cloud) or `http://localhost:3000` (self-hosted). The connector requires `instance_url` as an install field; it is never hardcoded.

The connector normalises dashboards into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff that honours `Retry-After`, and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::GrafanaHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Create a Grafana Service Account token

1. Sign in to your Grafana stack as an admin (e.g. `https://myorg.grafana.net`).
2. Open **Administration → Service Accounts**.
3. Click **Add service account**, give it a name (e.g. `shielva-connector`), pick a role (`Editor` for read+write or `Viewer` for read-only).
4. On the service account page, click **Add service account token**.
5. Name it, leave default no-expiration (or set one), and click **Generate token**.
6. Copy the `glsa_...` value — Grafana shows it only once.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Grafana**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `instance_url` | e.g. `https://myorg.grafana.net` or `http://localhost:3000` |
| `service_account_token` (secret) | Token from step 6 |
| `org_id` (number, optional) | Grafana org id — defaults to 1 |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 300 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates that both required fields are non-empty (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `GrafanaHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, CONNECTED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which probes `GET /api/health`. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `OFFLINE + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 403 → `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Grafana REST API uses **Service Account token authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). The token is sent as a `Bearer` value. There is no OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry (unless the operator sets one).

### Credential model

- `instance_url` — tenant-specific Grafana stack URL. Stored verbatim, only trailing slash trimmed.
- `service_account_token` — `glsa_*` token. Sent as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.
- `org_id` — optional. Used only as a returned identifier; not currently injected as `X-Grafana-Org-Id`.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `instance_url` + `service_account_token`. Initialises `GrafanaHTTPClient`. Does **not** call any Grafana endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Wraps the token in `TokenInfo(token_type="Bearer")`, persists via `set_token`. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /api/health` — minimal probe (no auth header strictly required, but token is sent). |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — Service Account tokens are non-expiring until revoked. |
| token storage | `TokenInfo` is saved via `set_token` for parity with OAuth connectors. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Authorization: Bearer <service_account_token>
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `instance_url` | string | yes | `self.instance_url` | Grafana base URL (trailing slash trimmed) |
| `service_account_token` | secret | yes | `self.service_account_token` | Bearer token |
| `org_id` | number | no | `self.org_id` | Grafana org id (defaults to 1) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 300) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
CONNECTOR_TYPE = "grafana"
CONNECTOR_NAME = "Grafana"
AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["instance_url", "service_account_token"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `GrafanaConnector`. SOC: `connector.py` orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::GrafanaHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `instance_url` + `service_account_token`; initialise `GrafanaHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — wraps `service_account_token` in `TokenInfo(token_type="Bearer")` and persists via `set_token`.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Probes `GET /api/health`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Lists all dashboards → fetches each full dashboard → normalizes to `NormalizedDocument` → calls `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Organization
children.content:
### `async get_org() -> Dict`
`GET /api/org` — current organization context for the auth token.

children.title: Dashboards
children.content:
### `async list_dashboards(*, limit=100, page=1, query=None, tag=None, folder_uids=None) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /api/search?type=dash-db` with optional `query`, `tag`, and `folderUIDs` filters.

### `async get_dashboard(uid) -> Dict`
`GET /api/dashboards/uid/{uid}` — full dashboard JSON including `meta`.

### `async create_dashboard(dashboard, *, folder_uid=None, overwrite=False) -> Dict`
`POST /api/dashboards/db` — body: `{dashboard, folderUid, overwrite}`. Creates or upserts.

### `async delete_dashboard(uid) -> Dict`
`DELETE /api/dashboards/uid/{uid}`.

### `async get_dashboard_doc(uid) -> NormalizedDocument`
Convenience: search by uid + fetch full dashboard, return a `NormalizedDocument` ready for KB ingest.

children.title: Folders
children.content:
### `async list_folders(*, limit=100, page=1) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /api/folders`.

### `async create_folder(title, *, uid=None) -> Dict`
`POST /api/folders` — body: `{title, uid?}`.

children.title: Datasources
children.content:
### `async list_datasources() -> List[Dict]`
`GET /api/datasources`.

### `async create_datasource(name, type, url, *, access="proxy", is_default=False, basic_auth=False, json_data=None) -> Dict`
`POST /api/datasources` — body: `{name, type, url, access, isDefault, basicAuth, jsonData?}`.

### `async query_datasource(datasource_id, queries, *, from_time=None, to_time=None) -> Dict`
`POST /api/ds/query` — body: `{queries: [{datasourceId, refId, ...}], from?, to?}`. Each query is automatically enriched with `datasourceId` + default `refId=A`.

children.title: Users · Teams
children.content:
### `async list_users(*, perpage=1000, page=1) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /api/users`.

### `async list_teams(*, perpage=1000, page=1, query=None) -> Dict`
`GET /api/teams/search`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
GrafanaError                          # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── GrafanaAuthError                  # 401 / 403
├── GrafanaNotFound                   # 404
├── GrafanaRateLimitError             # 429 — retry_after_s
├── GrafanaNetworkError               # transport failure (DNS, TLS, timeout)
└── GrafanaAPIError                   # other 4xx / unretried 5xx
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Honour `Retry-After` header; otherwise exponential backoff `min(1.0 * 2 ** attempt + jitter, 32)`, retry up to `DEFAULT_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` / `httpx.TransportError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `GrafanaNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   — logged as warning
network → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, CONNECTED)
other → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `instance_url` or `service_account_token` blank | Re-enter both in ACP. Grafana shows the token only once at creation — rotate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 — token role too low | In Grafana, edit the service account and raise role to `Editor` (read+write) or `Admin` (datasource provisioning). |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 — token revoked or expired | Generate a new service account token and re-install. |
| `grafana.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Grafana per-stack rate cap) | The client retries with `Retry-After`-aware exponential backoff. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer requests. |
| 404 on dashboard ops | Dashboard UID wrong or belongs to another org | Verify the UID in the Grafana UI URL; ensure `org_id` matches the dashboard's org. |
| `instance_url` mismatch | Pasted UI URL instead of stack URL | For Grafana Cloud use the API base — `https://<stack>.grafana.net` (no trailing path). |
| Datasource queries return empty `results` | Wrong `datasourceId` or query payload | Inspect the request payload — `datasourceId` is automatically injected, but `refId` must be unique per query; queries also need `expr`, `query`, or `target` per datasource type. |
